purpose: Tracks the competitor-driven enhancement loop (every 30m). One tool per iteration, rotating. Tax/paycheck calculator is OUT OF SCOPE.

history.shipped: Print-ready PDF export of the sheet (top backlog item). Added a 'Download print sheet as PDF' button (#dlSheetPdf) below the existing PNG/JPG photo + sheet buttons; enabled alongside them on file load. New downloadSheetPdf() reuses the shared tileSheet()/sheetLayout() helpers to paint the same tiled sheet (white bg, copies, grey cut guides) onto an offscreen canvas, then renders it into a jsPDF page sized to the sheet's TRUE physical dimensions: points = px / 300 * 72 (all sheet sizes are 300-DPI derivations), orientation derived from w>h, single addImage of the canvas as JPEG q0.95 at 0,0 covering the full page so the print is 1:1 at any lab. Filename `<specId>-<sizeId>-sheet.pdf`; status line reports the size label + photo count (same count source as the raster sheet). jsPDF is the already-vendored src/assets/jspdf.umd.min.js (invoice-generator uses it); loaded globally via a <script src> in the template before the module (mirrors invoice), guarded by a window.jspdf check that surfaces a refresh hint if it failed to load. build.js already copies jspdf.umd.min.js to dist/assets. Closes the top passport-photo-maker backlog item — PixConvert/Levo Tools offer JPG + PDF sheet export; we had PNG/JPG raster only (print labs/kiosks often prefer a fixed-size PDF). No new deps; no photo-specs.json changes (no data-sourcing conflict); dist gitignored, rebuilt at deploy.

Platform output-size presets. Added a 'Platform preset' <select> (#preset) above the existing custom Output-size select, with square-avatar dimensions every competitor ships: Discord 128, Facebook 170, TikTok 200, Instagram 320, X/Twitter 400, LinkedIn 400, WhatsApp 512, YouTube 800; default 'Custom size' falls back to the 256/512/1024 select. New outputSize() helper in circle-crop.js returns the preset px when one is chosen, else the custom select value; download() now calls outputSize() so the exported PNG/JPG matches the chosen platform size (width=height=size, framing unchanged). syncPreset() disables the custom #outSize select while a preset is active (visual lock — the preset overrides it) and refreshes the #status line to read 'Output: <label> · drag to reposition…' (label = preset option text like 'Instagram (320)' or '<n>×<n>' for custom); it runs on preset change, custom-size change, at init, and via setLoaded after an image loads so the readout appears once an image is present. Closes the platform-preset gap — circlecropimage.dev advertises 'Discord, Instagram, WhatsApp, TikTok… presets', Fotor markets per-platform PFP sizes, and most circle croppers expose them; we only had generic 256/512/1024. No new deps; canvas engine untouched (export geometry is resolution-independent already); dist gitignored, rebuilt at deploy.

Optional center-logo overlay (top backlog item). New #qrLogo file input (PNG/JPG/SVG) in the template plus a hint line with a hidden 'Remove logo' button (#qrLogoClear). qr.js: onLogoChange() reads the file via FileReader -> data URL -> decoded Image (fully client-side, nothing uploaded); logoDataUrl + logoImg are module-level state. When a logo is present render() forces error correction to High (ecc='H' overrides the #qrEcc selector) so the modules the logo covers don't break the scan, and reflectLogoState() visually locks the ECC <select> to High + disabled and reveals the remove button. drawLogo(ctx,dim,bg) paints a bg-coloured padding box (LOGO_FRACTION=0.2 of the QR dim, padding = size*0.14 each side) then drawImage's the logo centered, aspect-ratio preserved (min-fit into the 20% box). The SVG export (svgString) mirrors this in viewBox/module units: a <rect fill=bg> padding box plus an <image href=base64 dataURL preserveAspectRatio='xMidYMid meet'> so PNG and SVG match; added xmlns:xlink to the <svg>. clearLogo() resets state + input.value and re-renders; status line appends ' · logo (ECC: High)'. Closes the last qr-code-generator backlog item — QRCode Monkey/QR Tiger let you drop a brand logo in the center (needs forced High ECC + a cleared center), which we lacked. No new deps (uses the vendored qrcode lib + canvas/FileReader); dist gitignored, rebuilt at deploy.

Amount Paid / Balance Due fields (top backlog item). Added an optional 'Amount paid' number input (min 0) to the Totals section beside Shipping. totals() now reads it as t.amountPaid and computes t.balanceDue = total - amountPaid. When amountPaid is non-zero, the live preview renders an 'Amount paid' deduction row (−money) plus a 'Balance due' grand row below Total; when balanceDue <= 0 that row switches to the brand color and shows 'money(0) · PAID' as a fully-settled marker (handles overpayment too — never shows a negative balance label, clamps the display to PAID). The PDF mirrors this exactly in the totals block: after the Total line it draws a brand-color rule, an 'Amount paid' line, another brand-color rule, then either a bold 'Balance due' line or a brand-color 'Balance due  money(0)  PAID' line. Persisted: 'amountPaid' added to FIELD_IDS (autosave/restore) and the init input-listener array (live re-render); resetForm seeds it to 0. Closes the top invoice backlog item — invoice-generator.com (4M+ users) exposes Amount Paid / Balance Due at the bottom of its totals block for deposits/partial payments, which we lacked (users had to fake it). Pure totals math; no new deps; jsPDF unchanged; dist gitignored, rebuilt at deploy.

Live print-sheet layout preview with cut guides. Added a 'Preview sheet layout' toggle (#togglePreview, enabled once an image loads) that swaps the interactive single-photo editor (#editorWrap) for a new on-screen #sheetPreview canvas rendering the full tiled sheet (white bg, all copies, grey cut-guide rectangles) exactly as it will print — so the user sees the layout BEFORE downloading. Plus a live #sheetInfo readout under the sheet-size selector ('Fits N photos (cols x rows) with cut guides.') that updates with no image needed. Refactored the download tiling into three shared helpers: sheetLayout(ps) returns {cols,rows,gapX,gapY,count} (max copies edge-to-edge, leftover spread as even gaps), tileSheet(ctx,ps,bmp) paints the sheet, and renderSheetPreview() draws into #sheetPreview at full sheet px then CSS-scales to MAX_DISPLAY_H; downloadSheet now reuses tileSheet/sheetLayout so the preview geometry is byte-identical to the export. Preview re-renders on zoom (slider), document-type change (photo dims change copies-per-sheet), sheet-size change, and on a new file load while previewing; updateSheetInfo() runs the same on those events and at init. setPreview(on) toggles visibility + button label ('Back to editor') + aria-pressed. Competitor parity: PassportGrid ('Show cutting guide grid'), Levo Tools (preview spacing + cut guides), 123PassportPhoto (cut along grey lines), Cutout.Pro all preview the cut-guide tiling before printing — we drew cut guides into the export but never previewed them. Closes the 'grid-line / cut-guide preview' backlog item. No new deps; pure canvas; no photo-specs.json changes (no data-sourcing conflict). dist gitignored, rebuilt at deploy.

JPG download option alongside PNG. Added a 'Download format' <select> (PNG transparent / JPG smaller, white background) to the circle-crop template, plus a #jpgNote hint shown only for JPG. download() now reads the format: PNG keeps the existing transparent export; JPG calls toBlob with type image/jpeg, quality 0.92, and a new `flatten` color so the alpha-less corners of the circle don't render black — flatten uses the user's chosen background color when 'Fill background' is enabled, otherwise white. Filename/ext + status now reflect the chosen format; new syncFormat() updates the button label ('Download JPG'/'Download PNG') and toggles the JPG note. Engine change: CanvasEditor.toBlob accepts an optional `flatten` CSS color that paints an opaque backdrop over the whole output canvas before _drawTo runs (PNG path and on-screen preview unaffected — flatten defaults to null). Closes the last small backlog item for this tool (Fotor offers PNG-transparent vs solid JPG output; we were PNG-only); background fill shipped previously unblocked it. No new deps; dist gitignored, rebuilt at deploy. The only remaining backlog idea is batch mode, which is too large for a micro-commit.

Low-contrast / scannability warning when foreground and background colours are too close. render() now calls updateContrastWarning(fg, bg), which computes the WCAG relative-luminance contrast ratio (hexToRgb -> relLuminance -> contrastInfo, range 1:1..21:1) and shows an amber #qrWarn alert under the preview in two cases: (1) foreground lighter than background (inverted — many scanners reject dark-bg/light-fg outright), and (2) ratio below MIN_CONTRAST=4, the practical ISO/IEC 18004 floor for reliable smartphone scans (black-on-white is 21:1). The warning auto-clears (hidden) when contrast is adequate and silently hides if either colour can't be parsed. New #qrWarn

in the template (hidden by default, amber inline style). Pure colour math — no new deps, no logic change to the QR encoding/export. Closes the low-contrast backlog idea; competitors like qqrcodegenerator ship a dedicated QR contrast checker and pageloot/gocreateqr publish 4:1-minimum contrast guidance we gave no feedback on. dist gitignored, rebuilt at deploy.

Shipping / handling line in totals. Added an optional shipping field (number, min 0) in the Totals section; totals() reads it as a flat pass-through charge added AFTER tax and discount (untaxed) into total = max(0, subtotal + tax - discount + shipping), and exposes it on the model (t.shipping). It renders as a 'Shipping' row in the live preview (only when non-zero) and in the PDF totals via the existing line() helper, sitting between Discount and the Total separator. Persisted: 'shipping' added to FIELD_IDS so autosave/restore covers it; resetForm seeds it to 0; init wires it into the live-render input listener. Closes the top backlog item — Flowlu / Invoice-Generator.com expose a separate shipping line we lacked (previously shipping had to be faked as an extra line item, which got taxed). No new deps; jsPDF unchanged; dist gitignored, rebuilt at deploy.

Background readiness hint (corner-pixel check). After upload, checkBackground() samples the four corner patches (8% of the shorter side each) of the UPLOADED image via an offscreen canvas, averages each corner, then warns without altering anything when: inter-corner spread > 60 (busy/uneven — shadows, pattern, scenery), mean luminance < 110 (dark), or sum-of-channels distance to the spec background > 150 (wrong colour); otherwise a green 'looks plain and close to required' confirmation. New #bgHint

in the template (hidden until an image loads); hint re-runs on document-type change so it re-evaluates against each spec's required background (US white vs UK/AU light grey via hexToRgb(spec.background)). Tainted-canvas/decode errors hide the hint silently. Pure pixel stats — no AI, no background removal: US/UK/CA forbid digitally altering the photo background, so the compliant move is to advise the user to retake, not to remove. Closes the top backlog item and matches the 'validator/checker' feature competitors (PassportPhotoFactory's 15-check validator, iShotaPhoto's 20+ checks, Passlens) sell; background is the most common rejection reason. AI/WASM background removal stays deliberately out of scope (too heavy + non-compliant for US/UK/CA). No new deps; no photo-specs.json changes (no data-sourcing conflict); dist gitignored, rebuilt at deploy.

Rotate / straighten the image before cropping. Added CanvasEditor.setRotation (clamped -180..180 deg, default 0) composed into the shared _drawTo so preview and exported PNG match at any output size. The image is rotated about the box center INSIDE the circle clip; to avoid transparent corners when straightening, the cover scale is multiplied by the rotated-frame bounding-box factor (max of the two axis growth ratios) so the rotated image still fully covers the box at any angle. zoom/pan/padding/border all still compose. New rotate slider (-180..180, live degree readout #rotateVal, plus a 'Reset rotation' btn-secondary) in the circle-crop template; wired in circle-crop.js (setRotation on input, syncRotation on load/reset). rotation resets to 0 on a fresh image load. Closes the rotate/straighten backlog idea — Watermarkly offers a straighten slider before cropping that we lacked. No new deps; dist gitignored, rebuilt at deploy.

Adjustable quiet-zone (margin) selector — Standard (4 modules) / Narrow (2) / None (0). Replaced the hardcoded QUIET=4 constant in qr.js with a clamped quietZone() reader (0-8, default 4) driven by a new #qrMargin <select> in the template; both render() (canvas) and svgString() (SVG export) now use the live value so preview, PNG, and SVG all honor the chosen margin. The selector lives inside #qrForm so the existing input listener re-renders on change with no new wiring. Tighter/zero margins let the code embed flush in dense layouts; Standard stays the scan-safe default. Closes the quiet-zone/margin backlog idea (QRCode Monkey exposes a 'Set margin'/quiet-zone control we lacked). No new deps; dist gitignored, rebuilt at deploy.

Autosave to localStorage so a refresh doesn't lose work. render() and downloadPdf() now serialize the full form (business/client fields, invoice meta, currency, dates, tax/discount, brand color, notes, all line items, and the optional logo dataURL) to localStorage key 'tb.invoice.v1'; init() restores it via applyState() before first render (restoring flag suppresses re-save during repopulation, dates are only seeded on a truly fresh visit so a cleared date isn't re-filled). Added a 'Clear & start fresh' button (confirm-gated) that wipes storage and resets to the shipped defaults, plus an autosave reassurance line in the template. Also hardened itemRow to attribute-escape desc/qty/rate (new attr() helper) since restored values flow back through the same value="..." interpolation. Competitor parity: InvoiceSimple/Invoice-Generator/Zoho all persist a draft so reloads don't wipe input; we lost everything on refresh. No new deps; dist gitignored, rebuilt at deploy.

Selectable print-sheet size (4×6 in / 5×7 in / A4). Added a 'sizes' array to photo-specs.json printSheet (standard physical paper dims @300DPI: 4x6=1200×1800, 5x7=1500×2100, A4=2480×3508 — NOT country biometric specs, so no data-sourcing conflict), a sheetSize <select> in the template, and selector wiring in photo-maker.js (sheetSpec tracks the choice; downloadSheet tiles onto the chosen sheet; filename + status use the size id/label; falls back to the legacy single 4×6 when no sizes array). The generic cols/rows tiling already handled arbitrary sheet dimensions. Closes the paper-size-selector gap every competitor (PixConvert, MakePassportPhoto, Cutout.Pro, Passport Photo Snap) offers — we hardcoded 4×6. No new deps; PNG/JPG format selector unchanged.

Optional padding/margin around the circle (toggle + amount slider). Added CanvasEditor.setPadding, which shrinks the shape (and its border ring, image clip, and bg fill) inward by a fraction of the half-size, leaving a transparent margin so the avatar isn't flush to the edge. Implemented in the shared _drawTo so preview and exported PNG match at any output size; pad cascades through outerR -> inset -> innerR. FrameToolkit explicitly offers 'add padding' around the circle; this closes that gap. No new deps; source only (dist is gitignored, rebuilt at deploy).

SEO/copy fix: the page already supports 7 payload types (URL, WiFi, vCard, Email, SMS, Phone, Text) but the <title>, meta description, og:title/og:description, and JSON-LD description still only advertised 'WiFi, vCard & URL'. Updated all four to enumerate the full set, recovering long-tail search coverage (email/sms/phone/text QR) competitors rank for. No JS/logic change; content-only.

Brand color picker. A color input (defaulting to the site accent #1a7f37, with a Reset button, hex-validated) recolors the INVOICE heading and the grand-total separator rule in both the live preview and the exported PDF (jsPDF setTextColor/setDrawColor). Closes the brand-color gap competitors like SmoothLedger and InvoiceSimple offer; no new deps.

Percentage discount option. The discount field now has a type selector (amount | %); a percent discount is applied to the subtotal pre-tax, and the percent is labelled in both the live preview and the PDF totals. Closes the amount-only gap competitors don't have.

Optional logo upload (PNG/JPG) — client-side FileReader, shown in live preview and embedded in the PDF header via jsPDF addImage. Competitor parity (logo/branding was the most common feature we lacked).

Foreground/background color pickers (HTML color inputs). Applied to the canvas render, the preview holder background, and the SVG export. Color customization was the universal feature every competitor had and we lacked.

Four new payload types: Email (mailto: with subject/body), SMS (SMSTO:number:message), Phone (tel:), and Plain text. Added options to the type selector, dedicated input groups in the template, and encoders in buildPayload(); also widened the input listener to cover textareas so the new multiline fields trigger live re-render. Email/SMS/phone/text are universal across competitors (QR Dex, the-qrcode-generator) and were our biggest payload-type gap.

Optional circular border/ring (toggle + color picker + thickness slider). Implemented in the shared CanvasEditor (_drawTo): thickness is a fraction of the radius so preview and exported PNG match at any output size; the image is clipped inside the ring. Avatar-style borders were a common competitor feature (Watermarkly et al.) we lacked.

Compliance-style head-position guide. Replaced the single averaged oval with the actual allowed head-height band: a crown line, a shaded chin band spanning the spec's headMin–headMax mm, crown/chin labels, plus a post-upload instruction stating the required head-height range. No new deps; SVG-only (no face detection, no fabricated specs). Competitors (PhotoGov, Pic4Pass, PersoFoto) all sell head-position compliance feedback we lacked; AI background removal was deliberately skipped as too heavy for a micro-commit.

Background fill option (toggle + color picker). When enabled, the transparent area behind the crop is filled with a solid color; for the circle shape only the inner disc is filled so the corners stay transparent (PNG alpha preserved). Implemented in the shared CanvasEditor _drawTo so preview and exported PNG match at any output size. Fotor offers PNG-transparent vs solid-fill output; this closes that gap and is the prerequisite for a future JPG export.

JPG output option alongside PNG. Added a download-format selector (PNG lossless / JPG); the choice is wired through both the single-photo and 4x6-sheet exports via CanvasEditor.toBlob type+quality. JPG is opaque because the rect editor already fills the frame with the spec background (white/light-grey), so no alpha-flattening fix was needed. Closes the JPG gap every competitor (PixConvert, PhotoGov) offers — print labs/kiosks prefer JPG. No new deps, no spec-data changes.

invoice-generator: Payment-terms presets (Net 15 / Net 30 / Net 60 / Due on receipt) — Wise/Zoho/InvoiceSimple ship a dropdown that auto-fills the due date from the invoice date; we have a free-text due date only. Small: a <select> that sets dueDate = invDate + N days and can drop a matching line into Notes.
Extend brand color to the table-header underline + BILL TO/DATE labels for fuller theming
Per-line-item tax toggle / tax-exempt lines (Invoice-Generator.com, Zoho) — shipping is already untaxed; this would let individual goods lines opt out of tax
qr-code-generator: Module/dot style (rounded dots vs square modules) — QRCode Monkey / QR Tiger offer a 'body shape' picker; currently we draw square fillRects only. Small-medium: a <select> (square/rounded/dots) that swaps fillRect for rounded-rect/arc in render() and matching rx/circle in svgString().
Logo size slider (10-25%) — let users tune LOGO_FRACTION; currently fixed at 20%. Tiny once the logo overlay exists, but only worth it if users ask.
Batch/bulk generation from a pasted list of URLs (QR Tiger bulk) — too large for a micro-commit; needs a textarea-to-N-codes UI + zip download.
crop-image-into-circle: Alternate crop shapes (square, rounded-rect/rounded-square via corner-radius slider) — IMG Cropper / Adobe Express / Fotor offer multi-shape crop incl. rounded square. The engine already supports shape:'rect'; small-medium: a shape <select> (circle/square/rounded) + a corner-radius slider that adds a rounded-rect clip path in _drawTo. Tool is branded 'circle' so frame as a secondary option, not the default.

Oval / ellipse crop option — circlecropimage.dev and several croppers offer an oval when a face/logo needs more vertical room than a perfect circle; engine would need a non-square clip (radiusX != radiusY) in the circle branch of _drawTo.

Flip / mirror the image before cropping (horizontal + vertical) — common editing affordance; a thin CanvasEditor.setFlip(h,v) that negates the transform inside _drawTo, mirroring rotate/straighten which already shipped.

Batch mode — crop multiple images to circles at once (Watermarkly, imagy) — too large for a single micro-commit; would need multi-file upload, a results grid, and per-file framing or a shared transform + zip download
passport-photo-maker: Compliance summary panel — combine the head-height band + background hint into a single pass/fail checklist (parity with PassportPhotoFactory's 15-check / iShotaPhoto's 20+ check validators)
More country specs (40+ on PixConvert / 60+ elsewhere) — only add with authoritative government sources; escalate conflicts per data rule

AI/WASM background removal to white — heavy (in-browser model) AND non-compliant for US/UK/CA (digital alteration forbidden; State Dept auto-rejects AI-altered photos since Jan 2026); the background readiness HINT (shipped) is the compliant alternative, so this is out of scope
